reviews.review_description:
If the sneak peek of the archery mechanic is representative of the overall gameplay we can expect, then a lot more work needs to be done between now and July 20.

I was playing with the Gear VR controller, and throughout the entire experience I would never have guessed it. Tracking is non-existent, and even the trigger didn't work for selection. Swiping horizontally was useless when browsing between tabs.

The archery section, the only playable part of this "sneak peek", controls worse. Holding down the trigger as instructed registered as if it were a tap. The bow was drawn and immediately shot the arrow out wherever you were looking at. No reticle appeared, and no time to aim. 

This is incredibly frustrating. It's clear that your art direction is amazing, but the haphazard level of support on the controls side completely negates that. Considering how close you are to your target date, I have little confidence that the final game will be much fun to play unless your devs hustle to fix this issue.

I sincerely hope you receive this review constructively. Gear VR needs a really good dungeon crawler, and if you can address the technical issues, this game has the graphics to potentially be that. I'm happy to update my review if the issues get fixed.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your detailed review. Actually the controls via Gear VR controller is our #1 task right now to work on and we're confident to offer a pleasant integration for launch of the full game.

reviews.review_description: Just assorted teasers for a future game.
I'm in the center, just look around 360, and shoot the flying skull. That's it. And I don't know how to go next stage. I don't know how long, how many shoot the skulls. What the f~!
when this summer is game being released ?

developer_response.body: If all goes well the full game is going to be released on July 20th already! :)
